title: Spectator ion
definition: An ion that appears unchanged on both sides of the complete-ionic equation — present before and after, never consumed. Cancelling the spectators is exactly what turns the complete-ionic equation into the net ionic one. They do not vanish: $\text{Na}^+$ and $\text{Cl}^-$ stay dissolved while $\text{Ca}^{2+}$ and $\text{PO}_4^{3-}$ leave solution as solid — they simply sit the reaction out.
